summary: All 6 acceptance criteria verified. 26 circuit-breaker tests pass. 384 total tests pass in opencode-plugin. TypeScript clean. Biome clean.
documentationFeedback: No user-facing docs updated (appropriate for internal plugin hook). Code has good JSDoc comments. PR body is adequate.
observations: No config merge tests for mergeCircuitBreaker specifically (minor gap, trivial function)
No integration test for wiring in index.ts (mitigated by TypeScript type checking)
Hook runs first in tool.execute.before chain, before subagentQuestionBlockerHook (correct ordering)

criteriaResults.criterion: TDD: failing test first, then implementation
criteriaResults.evidence: Implementer attestation in retro comment. Branch squash-merged so individual TDD commits not visible.

criteriaResults.criterion: 5 identical tool calls triggers the breaker
Warning/abort behavior configurable via plugin config
criteriaResults.evidence: abort throws, warn does not throw + console.warn called. Config wired at index.ts:94.
5 calls across 2 sessions does not trigger either. Sessions trigger independently.

criteriaResults.criterion: Tracking cleaned up on session.deleted event
criteriaResults.evidence: Counter resets after session.deleted. Other sessions unaffected.
bun test --grep 'does not trigger on 4' — 1 pass, 4 expect() calls

testCritique: Tests are meaningful — exercise real hook with real inputs, no excessive mocking. Good edge case coverage (null, nested, key order). Minor gap: no config merge test for mergeCircuitBreaker.
